detailedExemplarData.title: nvestigating The Correlation Between Batting Average and Strike Rate in Cricket

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Well-structured table of contents and clear sectional headings enhance navigation.
Introductionâmethodâanalysisâconclusion sequence is evident, aiding reader orientation.
Labels and organization largely consistent, with a concise descriptive title.
weaknesses: Transitions between some sections (e.g., from chi-square to regression) remain abrupt.
Excess narrative detail and duplicated calculator screenshots interrupt concise flow.
Minor repetition in âWork citedâ and âAppendixâ entries reduces polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent with clear organization (TOC, headings) and a logical overall sequence, meriting a band-3 score. However, abrupt transitions, narrative digressions, and occasional repetition prevent it reaching the highest band.

strengths: Effective use of multiple representations (contingency tables, scatter plots, regression curves) linked to the text.
Standard statistical symbols (r, ÏÂ², Hâ/Hâ) and formulae are present throughout.
Explanations are generally readable and the flow of mathematical ideas is maintained.
weaknesses: Inconsistencies in notation (mixed italic/roman, rounding practices) occasionally disrupt precision.
Some formulae are described in prose rather than displayed as block equations.
Labels on a few figures are missing or ambiguously placed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical communication is clear and mostly consistent, with effective graphical and tabular representations. Minor inconsistencies in notation and rounding justify a band-3 score rather than full marks.

strengths: Personal narrative in the introduction demonstrates genuine enthusiasm and context for the topic.
Choice of cricket statistics shows individual interest guiding question selection.
Evidence of initiative in applying three different statistical methods.
weaknesses: Analysis largely follows textbook procedures with limited adaptation to the cricket context.
Personal perspective informs topic choice but does not deeply shape analytical decisions.
Creativity in modelling is minimal; methods remain standard.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs personal interest drives the exploration and there is clear engagement, elevating this criterion above minimal performance (band-2). However, the methods are largely conventional, so it does not reach the highest band.

strengths: The student reflects on p-values exceeding Î± and compares strengths of different models.
Basic critique of significance-level choice and model fit is present.
Connections are made between statistical outcomes and batting performance.
weaknesses: Reflection is at times descriptive rather than probing deeper assumptions or implications.
Some misinterpretations (e.g., r vs rÂ², quadratic rÂ²) indicate limited critical depth.
Opportunities for exploring broader cricketing or methodological implications are missed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: There is more than surface-level commentary: the student engages in meaningful critique of model choices and statistical thresholds. This justifies a band-2 score for reflection, though it lacks the depth for the top band.

strengths: Multiple SL-level techniques (ÏÂ² test, Pearson r, Spearman rank, linear/quadratic regression) are relevant and effectively applied.
Calculations are mostly correct with only minor rounding or interpretation inaccuracies.
Conceptual explanations demonstrate good understanding of each methodâs purpose.
weaknesses: Degree-of-freedom handling and expected-frequency rounding could be documented more rigorously.
Misinterpretation of r vs rÂ² suggests partial gaps in conceptual clarity.
Sampling stratification description omits exact stratum sizes, limiting methodological rigour.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The use of mathematics is effective and at course level, with mostly correct execution. Minor errors and conceptual slips prevent full marks, placing this in band-4 rather than the top two bands.

strandSelections.reasoning: Choosing cricket and designing the question shows personal interest, yet statistical methods are textbook selections with little original adaptation.  Evidence of independent choice beyond syllabus examples is limited.

Descriptions of each techniqueâs purpose demonstrate sound conceptual grasp, yet misunderstandings (comparing r with rÂ², meaning of independence) indicate that understanding is good only in parts, not thorough.

Calculator output suggests correct numerical results; however degree-of-freedom, expected-frequency rounding, and r vs rÂ² interpretation errors show minor inaccuracies.  Hence mathematics is âmostly correctâ rather than fully correct.

Core material is generally relevant, but lengthy verbal explanations of basic cricket terms, statistics, significance-level theory and copies of full calculator windows add unnecessary bulk.  Minor repetition occurs when results are quoted first from the GDC and again in text.  Thus the exploration is mostly relevant yet not consistently concise.

Standard symbols (r, ÏÂ², Hâ, Hâ) and formulae are shown, but formatting is sometimes inconsistent (mixed italic/roman, misplaced brackets) and variables are described in prose rather than notation in several places.  Overall use is acceptable but not âmostly appropriateâ throughout.

Explanations are readable, but switches between rounded and exact values, and between r and rÂ², create inconsistencies; some labels on figures are missing.  Hence clarity is mostly, but not fully, maintained.

Chi-square, Pearson r, linear and quadratic regression, and Spearman rank are all at SL course level and clearly relevant to the question.  Using several techniques constitutes effective application, but none extend beyond syllabus or reach sophistication.

The student employs tables, a 2Ã2 contingency table, two scatter plots with regression lines, and a quadratic curve; these multiple representations help illustrate findings and are generally well linked to text, meeting the âeffectiveâ criterion.

The student comments that the p-value exceeds Î± and that linear correlation is weak, but reflection remains surface-level and largely descriptive rather than probing assumptions or implications in depth.
Applying three different correlation techniques shows initiative, yet no novel mathematical idea or creative modelling is attempted; commentary is conventional.  Creativity is therefore limited.

The report follows an introductionâmethodâanalysisâconclusion sequence and ideas usually progress in a sensible order, yet transitions are sometimes abrupt and long narrative digressions (e.g., biographical remarks, copied GDC screens) interrupt the flow.  Logical links between sections such as why the chi-square precedes correlation are stated but not always convincing.  Overall, the reader can follow the argument with moderate effort, indicating some but not full coherence and development.

Basic critique is present (significance level choice, comparison of models) yet contains conceptual slipsâcomparing r with rÂ², mis-interpreting quadratic rÂ²âshowing only basic analytical critique.
Links between statistical outcomes and understanding of batting performance are asserted but not deeply explored; the connection to mathematical understanding is therefore limited.

A clear table of contents, correctly labelled headings, and separate sub-sections for each technique give the piece an easily recognisable introduction, body and conclusion.  Although screen-shot clutter weakens polish, the overall structure is orderly and information is located where expected, satisfying the descriptor for good organisation.

The narrative references childhood passion and justifies stratified sampling, but the analysis largely follows a standard recipe rather than a distinctly personal pathway.  Thus some, but not strong, personal approach is visible.

annotations.comment: The table of contents is comprehensive and well-structured, aiding navigation and reflecting clear organization of sections.
The title is concise and descriptive, clearly indicating the focus on batting average and strike rate correlation in cricket, which engages the reader effectively.
Consider adding the specific research question or hypothesis on the first page to immediately orient the reader and enhance coherence of the exploration.
The repetition of both âWork citedâ and âAppendixâ entries with the same page number could confuse readers; consider consolidating or clarifying these entries for conciseness.
The introduction contains engaging personal narrative, demonstrating genuine enthusiasm and personal connection to the topic.
The opening paragraph is overly narrative and diverts focus from the statistical aim; streamline context by reducing anecdotal detail.
Define variables symbolically before the formula, for example $$\text{BA} = \frac{\text{Total Runs}}{\text{Dismissals}}$$, to improve clarity and notation consistency.
The formula description uses prose rather than mathematical notation; present the formula in a single block equation for precision.
The inline formula $$y = 0.155612x + 82.8832$$ is displayed clearly, aiding mathematical understanding of linear regression.
The transition from chi-square to linear regression lacks a clear logical connector; explain why regression follows independence testing to improve coherence.
When rounding values (e.g., 0.155612 to 0.156), specify the rule (significant figures or decimal places) to maintain transparency and consistency.
The dataset rationale for restricting to ODIs is well justified, demonstrating sound judgment of data relevance and level.
Sampling description outlines stratification but omits the exact number of players per stratum; specify these to strengthen methodological rigour.
Hypotheses $$H_0$$ and $$H_1$$ are stated, but embedding them next to the expected vs observed table with mathematical notation would improve readability.
The observed frequency table is clearly formatted, supporting the chi-square calculation effectively.
Include the calculation of expected frequencies immediately beneath the observed table instead of in a separate section to enhance logical development.
The step-by-step expected frequency calculations (e.g., 10.7) use block math effectively, aiding the readerâs understanding of the chi-square process.
The aggregated expected frequencies in Table 3 (23.97 and 21.03) differ slightly from the row/column totals due to rounding; note this explicitly to avoid confusion.
Document the rounding rules used in frequency calculations (e.g., to one decimal place) in a footnote to maintain mathematical transparency.
